articles.title: Unseasonal rain in India: How long will it continue? Check IMD forecast on rainfall and heatwave - The Economic Times
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Delhi
articles.full_text:
Unseasonal rain, thunderstorms and hailstorms have been affecting several parts of India, raising concerns among residents and farmers alike. Based on the latest weather update, this unsettled weather is expected to continue for the next 3 to 5 days, with different regions experiencing varying intensity.
Rainfall Forecast India: Key Dates to Watch
According to current forecasts:
- April 8: Peak activity with widespread rain, thunderstorms and hailstorms across north, east and central India
- April 9–10: Continued scattered to moderate rainfall in many regions
- April 11 onwards: A fresh western disturbance may again bring rain, especially in northern regions
Overall, rain and storm activity is likely till around April 11–12, after which conditions should gradually stabilise in most areas.
North India Weather: Rain, Thunderstorms and Hail Alert
States like Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Punjab, Haryana, Delhi and Uttar Pradesh are seeing:
Rainfall will reduce after April 9, but another weather system may bring fresh showers from April 11.
East India Weather Update: Strong Winds and Heavy Rain
Eastern states including West Bengal, Bihar, Jharkhand and Odisha are experiencing:
Rain will continue till April 10, with intensity gradually decreasing after that.
Northeast India Rain Forecast: Wet Spell for 5 Days
The northeast region (Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Tripura, etc.) is likely to see:
- Heavy rain in isolated areas on April 9–10 and April 12
This region will remain the most consistently affected.
Central India Weather: Short Spell of Rain
Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh will see:
Conditions are expected to improve quickly after April 9.
Why Is This Unseasonal Rain Happening?
Multiple weather systems are active at the same time, including:
- A western disturbance over north Pakistan and Jammu & Kashmir
- Cyclonic circulations over Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan and Chhattisgarh
- An east-west trough stretching across northern India
These combined systems are causing widespread instability in the atmosphere.
Temperature Outlook: Heat to Return Soon
- Temperatures will remain normal or slightly below normal for the next 3–4 days
- After April 10–11, temperatures will rise sharply by 4–10°C across many regions
- Heatwave-like conditions may begin to build again in parts of the country
When Will Rain Stop?
- Most regions: Rain likely till April 10–12
- North India: Brief pause, then fresh rain from April 11
- Northeast: Rain continues for up to 5 days
In simple terms, expect a few more days of unstable weather, followed by a gradual return of heat.
